od_description: Soura ↔ Lal Mandi / Lal Chowk (central Srinagar), via the old city (Nowhatta – Gojwara). A north–south city spine.
stops_verdict: Real, high-traffic stop set. Top stops are genuine boarding points/terminals (busiest 34.07494,74.82846 = 4066 visits / 45 drivers ≈ TRC/Lal Chowk terminal; Soura end 34.1366,74.803 = 2500/29). A few high-dwell points (dwell 341s @34.12044,74.80795; 190s @34.08528) are likely layover/terminal dwells, not boarding stops. Overwhelmingly strong (many distinct drivers) — corroborates and enriches the register here.
narrative: The busiest observed corridor. Runs Soura (NE Srinagar, SKIMS area) south through the old city (Nowhatta/Gojwara) to Lal Mandi / Lal Chowk. Geometry overlap alone ranks 'Srinagar to Ganderbal' and 'Rangpora to Dalgate' highest (0.73), but those share only the central trunk and terminate elsewhere — the map shows the observed line does NOT continue north to Ganderbal. The correct match by endpoints is FDR-050 'Soura to Lalchowk' (0.71). JKRTC's e-bus chart independently lists Route 4 'TRC↔Soura via Nowhatta/Gojwara', the same corridor, confirming it is a real, major route.
evidence_used: geocoded endpoints: Soura (terminal A), Lal Mandi (terminal B), Nowhatta (mid)
candidate permit FDR-050 'Soura to Lalchowk' — exact endpoint/name match
map: observed green line ends at Soura, does not extend to Ganderbal (rules out FDR-288)
web (JKRTC/Kashmir Life e-bus chart): Route 4 TRC↔Soura via Nowhatta/Gojwara = same corridor
data_quality_flags: implied_headway 449 min is an artifact of partial app adoption (observed runs only), NOT real service frequency
effective speed 12.5 km/h includes ~38% dwell; moving speed 20 km/h is the cleaner travel measure
analyst_note: Demonstrates why AI matching beats the overlap threshold: correct permit is the 0.71, not the 0.73.
